div1.entryFree: Tenour (O. Edd. tenor, and oftener tenure) sense contained, purport: Gent. III, 1, 56. Meas. IV, 2, 216. Ado IV, 1, 169. Merch. IV, 1, 235. As IV, 3, 11. Wint. V, 1, 38. H4A IV, 4, 7. H4B IV, 1, 9. V, 5, 75. H5 V, 2, 72 (pl.). H8 I, 2, 206. Troil. II, 1, 100. Caes. IV, 3, 171. Cymb. II, 4, 36. III, 7, 1. Per. I, 1, 111. III Prol. 24. here folds she up the t. of her woe, Lucr. 1310 (i. e. her letter).* to find out shames and idle hours in me, the scope and t. of thy jealousy, Sonn. 61, 8 (== the aim and substance of etc.). misuse the t. of thy kinsman's trust, H4A V, 5, 5 (the meaning, the intention which he had in confiding the matter to thee).
